other_observers: Cathy Sheeter, who originally located the bird, exclaiming, "I see a dinky, dark goose!";
description: Tiny dark white-cheeked goose. Dwarfed by even Lesser Canada Geese (Bc parvipes) and smaller than nearby Richardson's Cackling Geese (Bh hutchinsii). Head rounded, delicate, bill small and stubby. When bird's head up and not preening, contour of bill smoothly transitioned into angle of forehead, not abrupt change as in Richardson's or somewhat angled, as in Aleutian Cackling Goose (Bh leucopareia). Neck and head black with white cheek patch that lacked indentation typical of Richardson's. Partial narrow gular stripe. At base of neck, anteriorly, thin white ring for about 20 percent of neck's total circumference. Breast shiny deep bronze, quite dark. The remainder of underparts similar in color, but a bit paler with some flank barring. Wing coverts highly patterned (for white-cheeked goose) with each feather having blue-gray base, dark brown subterminal band and crisp white terminal band. Back brown with narrow white bars. Undertail coverts and vent white. Bill and legs black.Behaviors: Sleeping on ice, occasionally preening or looking about, standing with Richardson's Cackling, Lesser Canada, and Moffitt (mostly Moffitt, presumably some Giant Canada genes as we are in CO) Canada Geese.Call: not heardPlumage: adult
photographer: Photographer: Steven Mlodinow, Steven Mlodinow, Steven Mlodinow, Steven Mlodinow, Steven Mlodinow
